steps.title: Track the bead mechanism
steps.content: The passage says optical tweezers use high-powered light beams to trap and manipulate tiny particles, but prolonged exposure to that intense light can overheat and damage the particles. Researchers address that risk by attaching nano-sized glass beads.

steps.title: Focus the light away
steps.content: With beads attached, the light focuses on the beads instead of directly on the particles. The particles can still be maneuvered for study, but they avoid the direct heating that creates the overheating risk described in the passage.

steps.title: Reject unsupported benefits
steps.content: Choice A adds faster locating, choice C adds weaker light beams, and choice D says particles transfer absorbed heat to the beads. The passage supports none of those mechanisms. The answer is B.
